Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Plaintiffs Sixth, Seventh, Eighth, And Ninth Causes Of Action Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-15-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lauren C. Taylor, Esq. of Cholakian & Associates, T:650.871.9544 ext.212, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTs SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY AKA HOUSING, and EDWIN JAMORA's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Plaintiffs Sixth, Seventh, Eighth, And Ninth Causes Of Action Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint was adopted by the Court, as follows:

Defendant San Francisco Housing Authority's motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action. "[U]nless Labor Code provisions are specifically made applicable to public employers, they only apply to employers in the private sector." (Johnson v. Arvin-Edison Water Storage Dist. (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 729, 733 [public entity was exempt from wage and hour statutes, Lab. Code 510 and 512, requiring overtime be paid for any work performed in excess of eight hours in one work day and requiring specific meal periods]; see also California Correctional Peace Officers' Assn. v. State of California (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 646, 650-656 [wage and hour statutes and IWC wage orders do not apply to public employees].) Plaintiff alleges that defendant failed to pay overtime compensation. The cited Labor Code sections are not specifically applicable to defendant. Plaintiff's claim regarding 7 days of work similarly fails because Labor Code 555 makes 550, 551, 552 and 554 applicable only to "cities which are cities and counties and to the officers and employees thereof." It does not apply to defendant, which is a distinct entity independent of the City and County of San Francisco. (See Gov. Code 900.4 [separately defining "local public entity" as including a county, city, and a "public authority"]; 970(c) [same].) Plaintiff's judicial estoppel argument lacks merit.

The motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted without leave to amend as to causes of action 7 and 9 as conceded. The motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted without leave to amend as to cause of action 8. Plaintiff alleges that defendant failed to pay for all hours worked and cites Labor Code provisions that are not specifically applicable to defendant. Plaintiff does not allege that defendant failed to pay a minimum wage.

Plaintiff has 15 days leave to amend to plead wage claims per the FLSA or any other applicable statute or ordinance. The motion for summary adjudication set for October 28, 2019 is off calendar given that plaintiff intends to file an amended complaint.

The trial is continued to June 15, 2020 to allow discovery on the proposed new claims and to allow defendant to renew its motion for summary judgment or adjudication.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
